citations.narrative:
In a cage containing two young guinea pigs there was a hole approximately 2.5 inches in diameter chewed through the
full thickness of the plastic material of the bottom pan of the cage as well as 4 chewed areas around the big hole that
were not full thickness. In the center of the large hole, the underlying plastic layer is also chewed. The top edge of the
bottom pan is chewed creating a rough edge and almost connecting with the large hole. The chewed areas are no longer
smooth and substantially impervious to liquids and moisture and are no longer able to be sanitized. The holes could allow
for escape or entrapment injuring the guinea pigs. All primary enclosures shall be maintained in good repair or replaced to
protect the guinea pigs from injury and be impervious to liquids and moisture and sanitizable.
To be corrected by 3/10/2026.

The bottom pan of the cage containing two rabbits has several holes chewed through the plastic material with exposed
wood underneath. One hole is about 1 inch in diameter and the other is approximately 2 inches in diameter. The top edge
of the bottom pan is chewed creating a rough edge. The exposed wood is chewed. The chewed areas are no longer
smooth and able to be readily sanitized and the exposed wood is not substantially impervious to moisture or sanitizable.
All interior surfaces shall be maintained or replaced so that they are substantially impervious to moisture and may be
readily sanitized.
To be corrected by 3/10/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative.
n

Primary enclosures. 
In a cage containing two young guinea pigs there was a hole approximately 2.5 inches in diameter chewed through the

3.51(d) 
   
Facilities, indoor. 
The bottom pan of the cage containing two rabbits has several holes chewed through the plastic material with exposed 
wood underneath. One hole is about 1 inch in diameter and the other is approximately 2 inches in diameter. The top edge

All interior surfaces shall be maintained or replaced so that they are substantially impervious to moisture and may be 
readily sanitized.

This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representative.
